content.content:
{"body":"
NEW TERM
peroxisome-chloroplast membrane tether activity
The binding activity of a molecule that brings together the peroxisome and the chloroplast membrane, establishing the localization of the peroxisome close to the chloroplast.
PMID:17215364

* **GO term ID and Label**
GO:0010381  peroxisome-chloroplast membrane tethering
The attachment of a peroxisome to a chloroplast via molecular tethers that physically bridge their respective membranes and attach them to each other. The tethering may facilitate exchange of metabolites between the organelles. PMID:17215364


* **Reason for deprecation** Put an x in the appropriate box: 

 - [x] The reason for obsoletion is that this term represents a molecular function.


* **"Consider" term(s) (ID and label)**
Suggestions for reannotation

NEW Term above, this is a MF

* **Are there annotations to this term?**
- How many EXP: 1 (TAIR)

* **Are there mappings and cross references to this term? (InterPro, Keywords; check QuickGO cross-references section)**

* **Is this term in a subset? (check the AmiGO page for that term)**

* **Any other information**


----
Checklist for ontology editor

***Check term usage and metadata in Protégé***
- [ ] check term usage in the ontology 
- [ ] check internal mappings: RHEA, EC, MetaCyc
- [ ] check subset usage
- [ ] check taxon constraints 

***Check annotations***
- [ ] create Annotation Review spreadsheet 
- * Annotation Review template: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1izV3q1jnq_Ybg5MJ53O_b5BCTkGtjl_Fi-cOg3-7eBE/edit#gid=0
- * Instructions: https://wiki.geneontology.org/Annotation_Review_Request
- [ ] create annotation review ticket: https://github.com/geneontology/go-annotation/issues/new?assignees=&labels=annotation+review&template=annotation-review-for-obsoletion.md&title=Review+annotations+to+%5BINSERT+GO+TERM%5D
- [ ] find external mappings (via IEAS), include in obsoletion notice  
- [ ] tag curators in Annotation Review ticket. Responsible annotator for each group: https://github.com/geneontology/go-site/blob/master/metadata/group-contacts.csv

***Notification***
- [ ] create [obsoletion announcement](https://github.com/geneontology/go-announcements/issues/new?assignees=&labels=obsoletion&template=obsoletion-notice.md&title=Obsoletion+notice%3A+%5BGO+ID%3A+term+label%5D)
- [ ] announce to GO friends (go-friends@mailman.stanford.edu)
- [ ] paste the text in the ontology ticket
","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7_ZBd9","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"New term added in PR #31929:

Definition: "The binding activity of a molecule that brings together the peroxisome membrane and the chloroplast membrane, establishing the localization of the peroxisome close to the chloroplast." [PMID:17215364]

Follows the same pattern as other peroxisome-organelle membrane tether activity terms (GO:0160190 peroxisome-mitochondrion, GO:0160229 peroxisome-ER), placed under GO:0140177 membrane-membrane adaptor activity.

content.content:
{"body":"## Summary

Obsoletes `GO:0010381` peroxisome-chloroplast membrane tethering (BP), per request from @raymond91125 in #31877.

The term was flagged as representing a molecular function rather than a biological process. The replacement MF term `GO:7770065` peroxisome-chloroplast membrane tether activity was added earlier in #31929.

## Obsoletion details

Resulting stanza:

```
[Term]
id: GO:0010381
name: obsolete peroxisome-chloroplast membrane tethering
namespace: biological_process
def: "OBSOLETE. The attachment of a peroxisome to a chloroplast via molecular tethers that physically bridge their respective membranes and attach them to each other. The tethering may facilitate exchange of metabolites between the organelles." [PMID:17215364]
comment: This term was made obsolete because it represents a molecular function rather than a biological process.
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31877" xsd:anyURI
is_obsolete: true
consider: GO:7770065
```

Used `consider:` rather than `replaced_by:` because the migration crosses namespaces (BP -> MF), so existing annotations require manual curator review rather than automatic migration.

Removed from the term: `is_a: GO:0140056 ! organelle localization by membrane tethering` and the `attachment of peroxisome to chloroplast` synonym, per standard obsoletion practice.

## Annotation impact

Per the issue, there is 1 EXP annotation (TAIR). @tberardini has confirmed in the issue they will migrate the annotation to `GO:7770065` once it propagates to their database, and requested the issue be kept open until that is complete.

## Validation

- No other terms in `go-edit.obo` reference `GO:0010381`.
- SPARQL QC checks: all passed.
- ELK reasoning: completed without unsat classes.

## Checklist

- [x] PLAN
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION (ontology was valid before changes)
- [x] RESEARCH — N/A (obsoletion directly requested, replacement already exists)
- [x] TERM-SEARCH (verified no other references to GO:0010381; confirmed GO:7770065 exists)
- [x] DESIGN-PATTERNS — N/A for obsoletion
- [x] EDITS (followed obo-checkout / obo-checkin procedure)
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS (all `is_a` removed from obsolete term, as required)
- [x] SPECIALIZED-EDITS (followed /term-obsoletion skill)
- [x] METADATA (name prefixed with `obsolete`, def prefixed with `OBSOLETE.`, comment with reason, term_tracker_item, `is_obsolete: true`, `consider` pointer)
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION (SPARQL QC + ELK reasoning passed)
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION — N/A (no new references introduced)
- [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED

---
🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent**
- Model: `claude-opus-4-7`
- Agent harness: claude-code
- Triggered by: @raymond91125

{"body":"## Summary

Obsoletes `GO:0010381` peroxisome-chloroplast membrane tethering (BP), per request from @raymond91125 in #31877.

The term was flagged as representing a molecular function rather than a biological process. The replacement MF term `GO:7770065` peroxisome-chloroplast membrane tether activity was added earlier in #31929.

## Obsoletion details

Resulting stanza:

```
[Term]
id: GO:0010381
name: obsolete peroxisome-chloroplast membrane tethering
namespace: biological_process
def: "OBSOLETE. The attachment of a peroxisome to a chloroplast via molecular tethers that physically bridge their respective membranes and attach them to each other. The tethering may facilitate exchange of metabolites between the organelles." [PMID:17215364]
comment: This term was made obsolete because it represents a molecular function rather than a biological process.
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31877" xsd:anyURI
is_obsolete: true
consider: GO:7770065
```

Used `consider:` rather than `replaced_by:` because the migration crosses namespaces (BP -> MF), so existing annotations require manual curator review rather than automatic migration.

Removed from the term: `is_a: GO:0140056 ! organelle localization by membrane tethering` and the `attachment of peroxisome to chloroplast` synonym, per standard obsoletion practice.

## Annotation impact

Per the issue, there is 1 EXP annotation (TAIR). @tberardini has confirmed in the issue they will migrate the annotation to `GO:7770065` once it propagates to their database, and requested the issue be kept open until that is complete.

## Validation

- No other terms in `go-edit.obo` reference `GO:0010381`.
- SPARQL QC checks: all passed.
- ELK reasoning: completed without unsat classes.

## Checklist

- [x] PLAN
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION (ontology was valid before changes)
- [x] RESEARCH — N/A (obsoletion directly requested, replacement already exists)
- [x] TERM-SEARCH (verified no other references to GO:0010381; confirmed GO:7770065 exists)
- [x] DESIGN-PATTERNS — N/A for obsoletion
- [x] EDITS (followed obo-checkout / obo-checkin procedure)
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS (all `is_a` removed from obsolete term, as required)
- [x] SPECIALIZED-EDITS (followed /term-obsoletion skill)
- [x] METADATA (name prefixed with `obsolete`, def prefixed with `OBSOLETE.`, comment with reason, term_tracker_item, `is_obsolete: true`, `consider` pointer)
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION (SPARQL QC + ELK reasoning passed)
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION — N/A (no new references introduced)
- [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED

content.text: Now let me save this convention as a project memory so I don't repeat the mistake.

input.content:
---
name: Taxon constraint files - only edit the .tsv
description: When changing taxon constraints in go-ontology, only edit src/taxon_constraints/never_in_taxon.tsv; never_in_taxon.ofn and imports/go_taxon_constraints.owl are regenerated by post-processing
type: feedback
---

For taxon constraint changes in geneontology/go-ontology, edit ONLY `src/taxon_constraints/never_in_taxon.tsv`. Do NOT regenerate or hand-modify `src/taxon_constraints/never_in_taxon.ofn` or `src/ontology/imports/go_taxon_constraints.owl` as part of an editorial PR.

**Why:** @raymond91125 corrected this on issue #31877 (2026-04-25). The `.ofn` and `imports/go_taxon_constraints.owl` are regenerated from the `.tsv` by post-processing — committing locally regenerated copies pollutes the PR diff and conflicts with the pipeline's authoritative output.

**How to apply:** When the task is to add/remove taxon constraints (e.g., obsoletion cleanup), modify `never_in_taxon.tsv` and stop. Run `make travis_build` for validation if needed, but revert any changes the build leaves in `.ofn` or `imports/go_taxon_constraints.owl` before committing. The `/taxon-constraint` skill mentions the .tsv files but does not currently warn about this regeneration trap.
